source: Keep It Simple- Graph Autoencoders Without Graph Convolutional Networks

concepts.finding: Simple graph autoencoder (GAE*) without GCN layers: MLP encoder + inner product decoder. Shows GCN layers sometimes hurt due to oversmoothing. Linear graph autoencoder matches or outperforms GCN-based autoencoders on link prediction and node clustering tasks. Key insight: simplicity often outperforms complexity in graph AE design.
concepts.implication: For keyword co-occurrence graphs, a simple autoencoder (linear encoder + inner product decoder) may outperform complex GNN-based embeddings. Oversmoothing in deep GNNs can collapse keyword embeddings from different intent clusters.
concepts.open_question: Is a simple linear autoencoder sufficient for keyword embedding, or do GNN-based methods provide meaningful benefits for keyword-intent graphs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A simple linear graph autoencoder produces keyword embeddings with comparable or better intent cluster quality than GCN or GAT-based autoencoders.
